concerns.text: FR-003 ('System MUST frame all findings as ASSOCIATIONAL...') is anchored to US-3, but US-3 (Statistical Analysis) focuses on model execution (ANOVA, FDR, sensitivity). The requirement to frame findings as 'associational' is a reporting/interpretation constraint that logically belongs to the data ingestion/validation phase (US-1) where the observational nature of secondary data is established, or requires a new User Story for 'Reporting & Interpretation'. Currently, it is an orphaned constraint relative to the specific scope of US-3.

FR-005 ('System MUST restrict execution to CPU-only...') is anchored to US-1 (Data Ingestion). However, US-1 is strictly about downloading and validating variable presence. The compute constraints (CPU, RAM, runtime) are execution constraints for the *entire* pipeline (Preprocessing US-2 and Analysis US-3), not just ingestion. Anchoring a global system constraint to the first user story creates a coverage gap for US-2 and US-3, which also need to adhere to these limits.

SC-002 ('Total compute time is measured against the 6-hour... limit') is anchored to US-3 (Statistical Analysis). While analysis is the most compute-intensive part, the 6-hour limit applies to the *entire* workflow (Ingestion + Preprocessing + Analysis). Anchoring this global success criterion solely to US-3 implies US-1 and US-2 are exempt or irrelevant to the total time budget, which is a coverage mismatch.
